items.title: Jalen Brunson came up visibly favoring and holding his right knee after colliding with Harrison Barnes on that last Julian Champagnie 3. Noticeably limping on the next possession, and after another Spurs bucket, he's checked out of the game.

media.title: Julian Champagnie's hard work pays off with NBA Finals - Andscape
media.description: Julian Champagnie, undrafted in 2022 and waived in 2023, is now a starter for the San Antonio Spurs and set to face his hometown Knicks in the Finals.

items.title: Playing in the NBA Finals in NYC is a “dream come true” for the #Spurs’ Julian Champagnie, who had to “dig deep” to survive on Brooklyn’s blacktop courts.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Playing in the NBA Finals in NYC is a “dream come true” for the #Spurs’ Julian Champagnie, who had to “dig deep” to survive on Brooklyn’s blacktop courts.

“It's kind of eat or be eaten, kill or be killed, he said.

On the forward’s love for his hometown:

www.expressnews.com/sports/spurs...

media.title: 'How 'digging deep' in Brooklyn molded the Spurs' Julian Champagnie
media.description: The New York City native says it's a "dream come true" to face the Knicks in the NBA Finals with at least two games at Madison Square Garden.

items.title: Edwin Gonzalez, Julian Champagnie's coach at Brooklyn's Bishop Loughlin Memorial, on how excited he was watching Game 7:
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Edwin Gonzalez, Julian Champagnie's coach at Brooklyn's Bishop Loughlin Memorial, on how excited he was watching Game 7:

"When his shots didn't go in, I cursed at the TV. I was coaching him on the TV, Put a little more arc on your shots. You'll make the next one," he told me.

items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Spurs starter Julian Champagnie, who scored 20-points and was +16 in game seven, is making only $3M this season and $3M next season. 

Victor Wembanyama, arguably the best player in the world, is making only  $13M this season and $17M next season.

Julian Champagnie's improbable journey from being cut by Philly on Valentine's Day 2023 to reaching the NBA Finals came to mind as he held onto the ball at the end of Game 7.

"I love everybody in the organization," he said. "It's a great place to be.

items.author: NBA
items.body_excerpt:
Julian Champagnie (20 PTS, 6-10 3PM) played a HUGE part in the Spurs' Game 7 win tonight!

He joins Klay Thompson (2016) and Stephen Curry (2x) as the only players in NBA history to knock down 6+ 3PM in a Conference Finals Game 7 🚨

items.title: Julian Champagnie seemed very relaxed in the locker room before the game, but that's how he is pretty much all the time.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Julian Champagnie seemed very relaxed in the locker room before the game, but that's how he is pretty much all the time.

Still, he's coming up big in a pressure-packed setting with 17 points powered by 5 of 6 from deep in 23 minutes.

Also note that defensive rebound he snagged before his last 3.

items.title: Julian Champagnie:
items.author: Nate Duncan
items.body_excerpt:
Julian Champagnie:


Regular-season points per GAME: 11


First-quarter points tonight: 13


The only other player with such a low scoring average to score so much in a conference finals or NBA Finals first quarter (play-by-play era) is Meyers Leonard in 2019.

items.title: Julian Champagnie believes it's time for him and his teammates to "hit back" to protect Wemby from the Thunder's physical play:
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Julian Champagnie believes it's time for him and his teammates to "hit back" to protect Wemby from the Thunder's physical play:

"The guys hit him, they pull him, they grab his jersey, they're grabbing his arm when he's jumping...That's not basketball.”
